                                                                                              Click Approve
                                                                                                    │
                                                                              ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
                                                                              │                     │                     │
                                                                        Job auto-closes    Owner notified    Syncs to Property Tree
                                                                                                                          │
                                                                                                              Rent holdback (auto) — §10
                                                                                                                          │
                                                                                                              Monday payment run + statement

docs.title: Healthy Homes Evidence (TPS)
docs.text: Version: V2.6 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Purpose / Outcome
docs.text: Healthy Homes (and related compliance evidence) is stored correctly in Property Tree and reflected accurately in TPS compliance fields so agreements/renewals are correct.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text: 1) Gather existing evidence (reports, certificates, invoices, photos, product specs) 2) Store evidence in Property Tree docs → 03 Compliance (use consistent naming) 3) Enter/update TPS compliance section fields using evidence 4) If gaps exist, create remediation tasks in Tapi with owner approval plan 5) After remediation, file completion evidence in Property Tree and update TPS fields

docs.title: Smoke alarms / renewal-time verification
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

When a renewal is being prepared in TPS, also verify the smoke alarm section:

1) Check the latest service or test date from SATS (sats.co.nz) if the property is under a smoke alarm testing service 2) If there is no SATS-managed service, use the last verified routine inspection date where appropriate 3) Update the TPS smoke alarm fields so the renewal agreement reflects the latest known test/service information 4) If TPS shows a non-compliant item but evidence suggests the work was completed, confirm via Property Tree invoices or Tapi before changing the field 5) Where the TPS field asks who checked the alarms, agent may be the correct entry if the evidence came from the PM / inspection workflow rather than SATS

docs.title: Property Tree evidence cross-check
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Navigate to: Property Tree → property / tenancy → Documents

Use Property Tree invoices and other stored evidence to support TPS compliance updates when the live TPS record is incomplete or stale. This is especially useful for:

  • extractor fan installation dates
  • draught stopping work
  • other Healthy Homes remediation items that were completed but not reflected in TPS yet
  • component-specific Healthy Homes dates where later remedial invoices provide a more accurate completion date than the original report alone

Do not force every Healthy Homes field to use one blanket assessment date if the evidence shows different completion dates for different components.

docs.title: Quality checks
docs.text:
Evidence integrity

  • Evidence must exist in Property Tree for every compliance claim
  • TPS compliance fields must match the evidence — no "wishful" entries

What's Next

  • Compliance & Healthy Homes — full compliance playbook including remediation
  • Property Tree — Filing & Naming — naming conventions for compliance docs
  • TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup — verify property-level compliance fields

docs.title: Compliance & Healthy Homes
docs.text: Version: V2.6 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Ensure the property is compliant before tenancy start (where required) and remain compliant throughout the tenancy, with evidence stored in Property Tree and compliance fields correctly maintained in TPS for agreement generation.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text:
• New management onboarding
  • Pre-leasing readiness
  • New tenancy/renewal/extension where compliance statement is required
  • Compliance gap identified (missing evidence, incomplete work)

docs.title: Outputs (definition of done)
docs.text:
• Compliance evidence set stored in Property Tree docs (03 Compliance folder)
  • TPS compliance fields populated enough for agreement and compliance statement
  • Remediation actions created in Tapi (quotes/work orders) with approvals tracked
  • Ongoing compliance review cadence established (monthly audit or as needed)

docs.title: Stage 1 — Gather evidence and determine gaps
docs.text: Verified from video analysis

docs.title: Assessment providers
docs.text:
• Healthy Homes assessment: HomelyHomes (or similar contractor) conducts the on-site assessment and produces a detailed report covering heating, insulation, ventilation, moisture/drainage, and draught stopping
  • Smoke alarm testing: SATS (Smoke Alarm Testing Services) manages smoke alarm installation, testing, and certification. Check the SATS portal (sats.co.nz) under My Properties for service dates and expiry dates.

Compliance deadline

All rental properties must be Healthy Homes compliant since 1 July 2025. Any new tenancy or renewal requires a current compliance statement.

docs.title: Collect what exists:
docs.text:
• Healthy Homes assessment/report (from HomelyHomes or equivalent)
  • Insulation details and evidence
  • Heating specs and evidence
  • Ventilation/extractor evidence
  • Moisture ingress/drainage notes and remediation history
  • Draught stopping notes (where relevant)
  • Smoke alarm info — check SATS portal for last service date and expiry dates (not technically a Healthy Homes standard but operationally tracked alongside it, required since 2016)

docs.title: Document gaps:
docs.text:
• "Missing evidence" vs "Non-compliant / needs work"

Store in Property Tree: 03 Compliance

docs.title: Stage 2 — Update TPS compliance section
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis Populate TPS compliance fields so tenancy agreements and compliance statements can be produced correctly (even if some work is pending, ensure it is recorded accurately).

  • Use SATS (sats.co.nz) or the last verified inspection date for smoke alarm last-check data
  • If TPS appears out of date, cross-check Property Tree invoices or other compliance evidence before changing the compliance answer
  • The evidence trail in Property Tree is the supporting proof for any compliance claim entered into TPS
  • Healthy Homes dates may need to be component-specific if later invoices or completion evidence show different valid dates for different remediation items

SOP: Healthy Homes Evidence (TPS)

docs.title: Stage 3 — Remediation through Tapi
docs.text:
Cost thresholds

For routine work, seek owner approval first. Apply the quote-first guideline for jobs > $500 or uncertain scope.

If work is required: - create Tapi jobs with photos/notes - issue work orders and keep tenant/owner informed

Playbook: Maintenance Lifecycle

docs.title: Stage 4 — Ongoing tracking
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis - After each compliance job, file completion evidence (invoice + photos + certificate/report) in Property Tree. - Monthly/quarterly audit: sample properties to ensure compliance evidence exists and TPS compliance fields are accurate. - At each renewal: update the smoke alarm last-check date in TPS from the SATS website or the last routine inspection date. Verify smoke alarm expiry dates are still current.

QA: Monthly Compliance Audit

What's Next

  • Healthy Homes Evidence (TPS) — populate TPS compliance fields
  • Maintenance Lifecycle — create remediation jobs in Tapi
  • TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup — verify compliance fields support agreement generation

docs.title: Landlord & Property Setup
docs.text: Version: V2.6 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Bring a new landlord and property into your full stack with zero missing information: - Property Tree record (owner + property) is correct, - Property Tree docs folders exist and key documents are filed, - TPS is set up for leasing, compliance, agreements, and renewals, - access and key handling is documented, - compliance readiness is tracked (Healthy Homes and related).

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text: Management authority signed OR takeover date confirmed.

docs.title: Outputs (definition of done)
docs.text:
• Owner + property created in Property Tree with correct fee/disbursement settings
  • Property Tree docs folders created and linked in the property record
  • TPS property created; compliance section populated enough to generate agreements
  • Keys/access plan documented (including tenant-in-place viewings)
  • Any compliance gaps converted into Tapi jobs (quote/work order plan)
  • Owner welcome message sent (approval preferences recorded)

docs.title: Stage 1 — Intake (collect and verify)
docs.text:
Intake checklist (expand)

Collect (and store in Property Tree docs "01 Management & Owner Docs"):

  • Management authority / agreement (incl. spend threshold if present)
  • Owner identity/contact details + bank account details
  • Preferred communication method and approval style
  • Insurance details (if supplied)
  • Property details: chattels list, utilities notes, parking/access constraints
  • Current tenancy status: vacant vs tenanted; key dates; rent; bond status

Takeover from another agency

If taking over from another agency, add a takeover checklist:

  • obtain last ingoing report, latest routine report, bond details, key notices, compliance evidence
  • confirm any arrears/disputes and document current status

docs.title: Stage 2 — Create the Property Tree master record
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Follow SOP: Property Tree — Create Owner & Property

Complete: - owner record - property record - fees and statement/disbursement settings - establish the Property Tree property file structure (docs folders) - verify owner bank details against the bank statement before any owner payment can be made - set the normal owner statement preference: - email delivery - basic statement - contact type Owner - use the management agreement commencement date for Authority Start Date - use the management agreement spend threshold for Expenditure Limit - do not assume the default management fee is correct; check the agreement and include GST correctly in the effective rate - if address lookup fails for a new build or unit address, correct it manually before saving

docs.title: Stage 3 — Create/verify TPS property setup
docs.text:
Follow SOP: TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup

Populate: - property basics (so you can lease) - compliance fields (so you can generate agreements and statements) - landlord details required by TPS - later tenancy setup in Property Tree must still cross-check rent against TPS / the final agreement, because the management agreement can be stale

docs.title: Stage 4 — Access plan and keys
docs.text: Document in Property Tree property notes + "01 Management & Owner Docs": - key count and where held - lockbox info (if used) - tenant-in-place viewings constraints and preferred windows - contractor access protocol (contact order and times)

docs.title: Stage 5 — Compliance readiness (Healthy Homes and other)
docs.text:
Run the compliance playbook if anything is missing: - Compliance & Healthy Homes

Convert gaps into Tapi jobs (quote/work order) with owner approval plan.

docs.title: Stage 6 — Go-live QA
docs.text: Run onboarding checklist: - Execution Checklists

docs.title: Templates used
docs.text:
• Owner welcome email (Notice & Email Templates)
  • Onboarding checklist

What's Next

  • Property Tree — Create Owner & Property — set up the master record
  • TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup — configure TPS for leasing and compliance
  • Compliance & Healthy Homes — ensure pre-tenancy compliance

docs.title: Property Tree — Create Owner & Property
docs.text: Version: V2.7 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Purpose / Outcome
docs.text: A complete Property Tree record for the owner and property, with standard docs folders created so the property file stays consistent across the business.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text: New management authority signed OR new property onboard instruction.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• Owner legal name(s), email, phone, postal address, bank details
  • Management agreement details (fees, spend threshold if any)
  • Property details (address, chattels, access plan)
  • Any existing compliance/insurance documents

docs.title: Steps (field checklist)
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Field names

Button names may vary; treat this as the required data list.

docs.title: 1) Create Owner
docs.text:
Navigate to: Property Tree → Managements dashboard → Profiles → Add Ownership

Page title shown in the UI: Add Ownership

  • Create owner/client record
  • Enter and verify:
  • full legal names
  • email + phone
  • postal address
  • bank account
  • Verify the bank account against the owner's bank statement before any payment is ever made to that owner
  • Use Add Payment Information inside the ownership record to enter the payout account
  • Notes:
  • approvals preference (approve/decline/quote first)
  • any spend threshold from management agreement

docs.title: 2) Create Property
docs.text:
Navigate to: Property Tree → Ownership record → Add Property

Page title shown in the UI: Add Property

  • Create property record
  • Enter and verify:
  • correct property address
  • chattels list
  • access notes (keys/lockbox/tenant constraints)
  • compliance notes (what exists/what missing)
  • preferred contractors (if any)
  • If address lookup does not resolve correctly for a new build, unit, or recently updated address, correct it manually before saving
  • Use the management agreement commencement date for Authority Start Date
  • Use the management agreement for the Expenditure Limit
  • Property type is usually House or Townhouse

docs.title: 3) Apply financial settings
docs.text:
• Use the Ownership screen for owner statements / payment setup
  • Use the Add Property screen for authority, expenditure, rent, and property-level settings
  • management fee settings (per agreement)
  • disbursement rules and statement frequency
  • set owner statements to the normal operating preference:
  • delivery by email
  • basic statement
  • contact type Owner
  • do not assume the default management fee is correct; override it if the agreement is different
  • account for GST in the effective percentage where needed
  • do not add or duplicate fees casually just because a fee-entry area is visible; only apply the fee/disbursement settings that match the management agreement

docs.title: 4) Create Property Tree "property file" docs folders
docs.text: Create: - 01 Management & Owner Docs - 02 Tenancy Agreements - 03 Compliance (Healthy Homes + related) - 04 Inspections (ingoing/routine/outgoing) - 05 Maintenance (approvals, quotes, invoices) - 06 Notices & Key Correspondence - 07 Insurance & Certificates

docs.title: 5) Upload/link key documents
docs.text: Upload to 01 / 07 / 03 as appropriate: - management authority - insurance (if supplied) - compliance evidence (if supplied)

docs.title: Quality checks
docs.text:
Verify before closing

  • Bank details match the bank statement
  • Address correct
  • Fees correct for this management agreement
  • Docs folders exist
  • Any missing items captured as tasks

docs.title: Close-out
docs.text:
• Mark onboarding checklist complete

What's Next

  • TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup — configure TPS for leasing
  • Property Tree — Filing & Naming — naming conventions for uploaded docs
  • Compliance & Healthy Homes — check compliance readiness

docs.title: Property Tree — Filing & Naming
docs.text: Version: V2.3 Last updated: 2026-03-31

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Make Property Tree the complete, searchable property file.

docs.title: Folder structure (standard)
docs.text:
• 01 Management & Owner Docs
  • 02 Tenancy Agreements
  • 03 Compliance
  • 04 Inspections
  • 05 Maintenance
  • 06 Notices & Key Correspondence
  • 07 Insurance & Certificates

docs.title: Naming conventions
docs.text:
File naming format

Use: YYYY-MM-DD — <Doc Type> — <Short Description>

Examples: - 2026-02-24 — Tenancy Agreement — Signed — Smith - 2026-02-24 — Bond — Lodged Confirmation - 2026-02-24 — Inspection — Routine — Report - 2026-02-24 — Notice — Rent Increase — Effective 2026-05-01 - 2026-02-24 — Maintenance — Invoice — Plumber — WO12345

docs.title: Email filing
docs.text:
If a decision was made by email: - export PDF - file under 06 Notices & Key Correspondence - add short note in property record referencing it

What's Next

  • Property Tree — Create Owner & Property — set up the master record using these folders
  • Landlord & Property Setup — full onboarding playbook

docs.title: TPS — Property & Trade Me Setup
docs.text: Version: V2.4 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Purpose / Outcome
docs.text: TPS property is fully configured so you can run BookMe viewings, receive applications, run checks, generate tenancy agreements, and execute renewals/extensions.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text: New property onboarded OR ready to lease.

docs.title: Step 1 — Create the Property in TPS
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Navigate to Properties → + New Property and complete the setup fields.

docs.title: Rent / Move-in Costs
docs.text: Field Guidance Available Date First date a tenant can move in — from the management agreement or owner correspondence Rent Weekly rent amount Period Weekly or Fortnightly only — under the Residential Tenancies Act, a landlord can only ask for up to 2 weeks' rent in advance, so monthly is not an option In Advance 1 Week (weekly) or 2 Weeks (fortnightly) Bond 4 weeks' rent — this is the maximum general bond allowed Pet Bond 2 weeks' rent additional — apply if a tenant will have a pet at the property

docs.title: Property Details
docs.text: Field Source Unit / Street Number / Street Name Management agreement or Google Suburb / City / Post Code Management agreement or Google Notes/Details Only if specific notes are needed Alarm Details Alarm code from management agreement (if any) TRADEME ID Paste the Trade Me Listing ID here after listing — this enables BookMe inquiry forwarding

docs.title: Agent Details
docs.text:
• Always select McKenzie Lawrence as the Property Manager
  • When logged in as the VA, verify the agent is set correctly — it may default to your name

docs.title: Landlord / Owner Details
docs.text:
• Full Name: from the management agreement (individual, trust, or company name)
  • Email: landlord's email address

Ownership changes

Landlords sometimes change their ownership structure during a tenancy (e.g., move to a company or trust). Always verify the landlord name in TPS matches Property Tree, especially at renewal time.

docs.title: Step 2 — Property Configuration Tabs
docs.text: Verified from video analysis

docs.title: Pets
docs.text: Select "By Negotiation" (or "By Application") — since late 2025, NZ pet laws require landlords to consider all pet requests. You cannot select "No" outright.

docs.title: Smoking
docs.text: Always set to No for indoors — smoking is never permitted indoors at any property.

docs.title: Dwelling Type & Specifications
docs.text: Field Guidance Dwelling Type House, Townhouse, Apartment, etc. — most properties are House or Townhouse Bedrooms Bedroom count from listing or management agreement Bathrooms Bathroom count Car Parks Garage + off-street parking count Max Residents PM's judgment — roughly 2 per bedroom as a guideline (e.g., 2-bed = 4 max). The tenancy agreement specifies approved residents; more than approved = potential tenancy breach

docs.title: Images
docs.text: Upload property images — source from the Trade Me listing or request from the PM.

docs.title: Step 3 — Chattels
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

The Chattels section contains a checklist of items present at the property (approximately 21 checkbox items). Tick what is known to be at the property.

All chattels data entered here flows directly onto the tenancy agreement. For a comprehensive list, add: "A full list of chattels plus key sheet will be provided with the ingoing condition report."

docs.title: Step 4 — Liability
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

The Liability section determines who pays for utilities and maintenance:

Item Typical Setting Notes Power Tenant ~99% of properties. Rare exceptions where landlord pays (e.g., no separate meter) Gas Tenant If applicable Water Tenant (excess charges only) In Christchurch, water is not consumption-based unless usage exceeds ~600–700 litres/day, at which point excess charges apply. A clause in the tenancy agreement covers this. Gardens / Lawns Tenant Sometimes an owner takes responsibility for part of a garden, or a body corporate manages shared grounds in multi-unit complexes

docs.title: Step 5 — Compliance (Healthy Homes)
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

All properties must be Healthy Homes compliant (deadline: 1 July 2025). The compliance section covers:

  • Heating — adequate fixed heating in the main living area
  • Insulation — ceiling and underfloor insulation meeting standards
  • Ventilation — functioning extractor fans in kitchen, bathroom, and any rooms with a cooking/bathing/shower appliance
  • Moisture ingress and drainage — no unaddressed moisture or drainage issues
  • Draught stopping — external doors and windows reasonably draught-free
  • Smoke alarms — working smoke alarms (legally required since 2016, operationally tracked alongside Healthy Homes)

Transfer information from the Healthy Homes assessment report (e.g., from HomelyHomes or similar assessment provider) into the compliance fields. If any item is non-compliant, the remediation must be completed before the tenancy start date.

See: Compliance & Healthy Homes

docs.title: Step 6 — Insurance
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Enter the landlord's insurance details from their policy documents:

Field Source Insurance Type From landlord's insurance certificate Excess Amount From landlord's policy — this is the most important field for the tenancy agreement's insurance statement

The insurance information is included in the tenancy agreement and must be disclosed to the tenant.

docs.title: Step 7 — Clauses
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

TPS provides a clause management system with four sub-sections:

Section Content E-Bundle Standard TPS clauses Office Specific Property Partner custom clauses Property Specific Unique clauses for individual properties (parking restrictions, owner garden access, etc.) Total Tenancy Additional TPS package clauses

Toggle clauses on/off using the selection panel on the right. The PM will advise on property-specific clauses as needed.

docs.title: Step 8 — Attachments
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Upload supporting documents that will be included with or available alongside the tenancy agreement:

  • Healthy Homes assessment report
  • Owner's insurance certificate
  • Any other relevant property-specific documents

docs.title: Step 9 — Trade Me Listing & BookMe Link
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

  1. Publish the Trade Me listing with property photos, details, and rent
  2. Copy the Listing ID from the bottom of the Trade Me listing page
  3. Paste into the TRADEME ID field in TPS Property Details
  4. Confirm enquiries appear in the BookMe dashboard (test with a sample inquiry if needed)

See: Viewings & BookMe (TPS)

docs.title: Property Status Flow
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Status Trigger Available Default when property is created and ready for leasing Under Offer Automatic when an application is approved in TPS Occupied Manual change — update once the tenant has moved in

docs.title: Tenancy Agreement Generation
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

All data entered across the property tabs (details, chattels, liability, compliance, insurance, clauses) is used to generate the tenancy agreement. When you create or renew an agreement, TPS populates it from these fields.

See: Agreements & Signing (TPS)

docs.title: Quality Checks
docs.text:
Verification checklist

  • TPS property data accurate and complete
  • Compliance fields match the Healthy Homes assessment report
  • Insurance excess amount entered
  • Landlord/owner name matches Property Tree
  • Pet setting is "By Negotiation/Application" (not "No")
  • Agent set to McKenzie Lawrence
  • BookMe routing confirmed (Trade Me ID entered, test inquiry if needed)

What's Next

  • Leasing Lifecycle — start marketing and leasing the property
  • Viewings & BookMe (TPS) — set up viewings once enquiries flow in
  • Compliance & Healthy Homes — ensure compliance fields are backed by evidence

docs.title: End of Tenancy & Lease Break
docs.text: Version: V2.3 Last updated: 2026-03-31

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: End tenancies cleanly with clear instructions, outgoing inspection, bond processing, and full records. For lease breaks, follow your published “fixed-term lease break guide”.

docs.title: Triggers
docs.text:
• tenant ending at expiry / periodic notice
  • mutual agreement to end early
  • lease break request received in writing

docs.title: Outputs (definition of done)
docs.text:
• vacate pack sent and filed
  • outgoing inspection completed and filed
  • bond process completed (or dispute route started)
  • property relet or handed back to owner
  • lease break fee and ongoing rent obligations managed and documented (where applicable)

docs.title: Stage 1 — Confirm end details in writing
docs.text:
• confirm end date and key return process
  • confirm outgoing inspection timing

docs.title: Stage 2 — Vacate pack
docs.text:
Send vacate pack with: - cleaning expectations - rubbish removal - keys/remotes return - final inspection process - bond process outline

Template: Notice & Email Templates

docs.title: Stage 3 — Outgoing inspection
docs.text:
• schedule in Property Tree; sync to Inspection Express
  • compare to ingoing report
  • document tenant damage vs fair wear/tear
  • publish and file report

SOP: Inspection Lifecycle · Inspection Express

docs.title: Stage 4 — Bond processing
docs.text:
• lodge bond refund/transfer form or Bond Hub process as used
  • file outcomes into Property Tree

SOP: Bond Lodgement

docs.title: Stage 5 — Lease break (fixed-term)
docs.text:
Use the published guide and internal workflow:

Lease Break — Key Rules

  • Request must be in writing
  • Landlord approval required before proceeding
  • Vacate date becomes a commitment once relisted
  • Fee typically one week rent (tenant must check their agreement), payable on approval
  • Rent continues until replacement tenancy starts or fixed end date reached (whichever first)
  • Property relisted immediately; viewings ASAP; tenant should maximise access
  • Require 2–3 clear days vacant possession before new tenancy begins

docs.title: Handover
docs.text:
• if re-letting: start Leasing Lifecycle
  • if handing back: confirm keys, final statement, close property file with notes

What's Next

  • Leasing Lifecycle — if reletting, start the leasing process
  • Notice & Email Templates — vacate pack and related notices

docs.title: Renewals & Rent Reviews
docs.text: Version: V2.4 Last updated: 2026-04-01

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Secure owner decisions early, notify tenants promptly, and execute renewals/extensions cleanly with correct records in TPS and Property Tree.

docs.title: Trigger
docs.text: Fixed term end approaching or annual rent review cycle.

docs.title: Outputs (definition of done)
docs.text:
• owner decision recorded in writing
  • tenant notified and outcome recorded
  • TPS renewal/extension executed if applicable
  • Property Tree updated (dates/rent) and docs filed
  • rent evidence saved (Valua) when used

docs.title: Cadence (your current structure)
docs.text:
• ~90 days: landlord contacted with renewal options
  • aim to notify tenant promptly once landlord direction is received (~80 days target)
  • follow-ups ~42 days and ~14 days

Escalation Rule

No response after: 2 follow-up emails → 1 phone call attempt → final deadline email.

docs.title: Stage 1 — Weekly pipeline review
docs.text:
• maintain a "next 120 days" list (VA/PM)
  • schedule outreach and follow-ups

See: Weekly Operations for the weekly pipeline review process.

docs.title: Stage 2 — Rent evidence (Valua) when needed
docs.text:
• run Valua rent research and prepare a short recommendation
  • store evidence in Property Tree

SOP: Valua — Rent Research

docs.title: Stage 3 — Owner consultation and written decision
docs.text:
• present options (renew fixed, periodic, end at expiry)
  • capture decision in writing
  • if rent change: confirm timing and notice requirements before serving

See: Notices & Communications for notice requirements.

docs.title: Stage 4 — Entering a Rent Increase in Property Tree
docs.text:
Verified from video analysis

Once the owner has agreed to a rent increase amount, enter it into Property Tree before sending the formal notice to the tenant. This ensures the system entry isn't forgotten.

docs.title: Calculate the effective date
docs.text:
1. The Residential Tenancies Act requires at least 60 days' written notice for any rent increase
  2. Use timeanddate.com to calculate 60 days from today
  3. The effective date must be the first rent payment due date after the 60-day minimum — not the exact 60-day date itself. This aligns with the tenant's existing rent cycle and avoids partial payment amounts.

docs.title: Enter into Property Tree
docs.text:
1. Open the tenant's Tenancy Profile → Lease & Rent tab
  2. Scroll to the Rent Schedule section and click the (+) button
  3. In the Scheduled Rent Change dialog:
    • EFFECTIVE: use the calendar picker — the orange-highlighted boxes show the tenant's rent cycle dates (payment due dates). Select the first orange date that falls after the 60-day minimum.
    • AMOUNT: enter the new weekly rent amount
    • PERIOD: keep as Weekly (or Fortnightly if applicable)
    • DAILY RATE: auto-calculated
  4. Bond Increase section: legally you can increase the bond up to 4 weeks' rent alongside a rent increase — but in practice this is generally not enforced to avoid additional tenant frustration
  5. Click Save

After saving, the Rent Schedule shows the new row with the effective date, new amount, and percentage increase.

docs.title: Automated rent change reminder
docs.text: Property Tree's Automated Communications system sends a "Rent Change Reminder" email to tenants 10 days before the effective date, reminding them to update their automatic payments.

docs.title: Send the formal rent increase notice
docs.text:
After entering the rent schedule, generate and send the formal Rent Increase Notice to the tenant. The notice must include:

  • Tenant names and property address
  • Current rent and new rent amount
  • Effective date and percentage increase
  • Legislative context (60-day notice under the Residential Tenancies Act 1986)

Enter rent schedule first, notice second

Always enter the rent increase into Property Tree's rent schedule first, then send the tenant the formal notice. This order prevents the common mistake of notifying the tenant but forgetting to update the system.

docs.title: Stage 5 — Tenant comms and execution
docs.text:
• send tenant renewal offer once owner direction confirmed
  • track replies; chase per cadence

docs.title: Stage 6 — Execute in TPS and update Property Tree
docs.text: Verified from video analysis

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before creating a renewal in TPS, confirm you have:

  • Written confirmation from both the landlord and the tenant agreeing to renew
  • The rent increase already entered in Property Tree's rent schedule (Stage 4)
  • The formal rent increase notice generated and ready to attach

docs.title: TPS Renewal Wizard
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Properties → [property] → Agreements tab
  2. Click the three-dot menu (⋮) on the current agreement
  3. Select Renewal (use this 90% of the time — it combines Extension and Variation capabilities)

Extension vs Variation vs Renewal

  • Extension: renew for the same period with no changes to terms
  • Variation: modify specific terms (add a pet, change a condition) without a full renewal
  • Renewal: effectively a new tenancy agreement — can change terms, increase rent, and extend the period. Use this by default.
  1. Complete the 8-step wizard — see Agreements & Signing (TPS) for the full click-level walkthrough
  2. Verify all dates match across TPS, Property Tree, and the rent increase notice
  3. Upload signed renewal documents to Property Tree

SOP: Agreements & Signing (TPS) — full TPS renewal wizard walkthrough.

docs.title: Templates used
docs.text:
• landlord renewal email
  • tenant renewal offer + reminders
  • rent review summary
  • rent increase notice (attach formal notice letter)

What's Next

  • Valua — Rent Research — get market evidence for the rent review
  • Agreements & Signing (TPS) — generate the renewal agreement

docs.title: Valua — Rent Research
docs.text: Version: V2.3 Last updated: 2026-03-31

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1) Build 4–8 comparable properties (same area, similar bed/bath/condition) 2) Exclude outliers 3) Produce recommended range and suggested figure 4) Send owner summary (range + figure + 2–3 bullet rationale) 5) Save evidence export/screenshot into Property Tree docs (03 Compliance or a Rent Reviews subfolder)

What's Next

  • Renewals & Rent Reviews — use the evidence in the renewal process
